routes.//: Static assets — public, no auth (CSS/JS chunks served by Next.js).
Standard Approuter CSRF-token endpoint. The browser fetches this with a GET and includes the returned token in the x-csrf-token header on every state-changing request.
Read-only API + UI — User scope. settings is INTENTIONALLY omitted; it has its own Admin-scope rule below.
/api/settings — Admin only at the Approuter (first gate). Next.js re-verifies the JWT scope (defense in depth).
Everything else (chat, embeddings, page routes) requires User. CSRF is enforced for all non-GET methods — clients must include x-csrf-token after fetching /csrf-token.
